question: What is the Grace CPU's role in the GB200 system, and why is a CPU integrated alongside the Blackwell GPU in the same package?
findings: The Grace CPU features 72 Neoverse V2 cores, up to 512 GB/s memory bandwidth, up to 480 GB LPDDR5X memory, and 900 GB/s NVLink-C2C bandwidth for coherent CPU-GPU connectivity.
The GB200 NVL72 system includes 36 Grace CPUs paired with 72 Blackwell GPUs, with each compute tray integrating 2x Grace CPUs and 4x Blackwell GPUs connected via NVLink-C2C.
The GB200 NVL72 provides up to 30.5 TB of fast-access memory by combining up to 17 TB of LPDDR5X CPU memory with up to 13.5 TB of HBM3E GPU memory, enabled by the Grace–Blackwell integration.

The Grace Blackwell Superchip (GB200 NVL4 form) integrates 1 Grace CPU with 2 Blackwell GPUs, providing 480 GB LPDDR5X CPU memory at up to 512 GB/s and 186 GB HBM3E GPU memory at up to 8 TB/s per GPU, connected via NVLink-C2C.
The Grace Hopper Superchip established the precedent for the Grace CPU + GPU co-packaged design, integrating 1 Grace CPU with 1 Hopper GPU via NVLink-C2C with a combined TDP of up to 1000W.
